latest.title: Notice of Interim Results & Investor Presentations
latest.content:
6 Jul 2026 12:27
RNS Number : 1867L
Vanquis Banking Group PLC
06 July 2026
Vanquis Banking Group Notice of 2026 Interim Results and Investor Presentations
London - 6 July 2026 - Vanquis Banking Group ('the Group' or 'Vanquis') intends to release its
2026 Interim Results
on
Thursday 30 July 2026 at 07:00 BST
.
This will be followed by a
presentation to investors and analysts
by CEO, Ian McLaughlin and CFO, Dave Watts at
08:30 BST
. Attendees can register for the webcast at the below link:
https://webcast.openbriefing.com/vanquis-1h26/
Attendees can also register for the conference call at the below link:
https://www.netroadshow.com/events/login/1PeTHmoj9tWqK66dS8LzDs5LKPgi65hgA2O14
Or the Operator Assisted Dial-Ins are below: United Kingdom (Local): +44 20 3936 2999
United Kingdom (Toll-Free): +44 808 189 0158
United States (Local): +1 646 233 4753
United States (Toll-Free):+1 855 979 6654
Global Dial-In Numbers
Access Code:
790070
Ian McLaughlin and Dave Watts will also provide a
presentation via Investor Meet Company
on
Tuesday 4 August 2026 at 15:00 BST
. Questions can be submitted pre-event via your Investor Meet Company dashboard up until Monday 3 August 2026 at 09:00 BST, or at any time during the live presentation.
Investors can sign up to Investor Meet Company for free and add to meet VANQUIS BANKING GROUP PLC via the following link:
https://www.investormeetcompany.com/vanquis-banking-group-plc/register-investor
Investors who already follow VANQUIS BANKING GROUP PLC on the Investor Meet Company platform will automatically be invited.
Both presentations are open to all existing and potential shareholders.
Enquiries
Investors and analysts
James Cranstoun, Head of Investor Relations
james.cranstoun@vanquis.com
+44 (0) 7766 937 406
Media
Scott Mowbray, Head of External Communications
scott.mowbray@vanquis.com
+44 (0) 7834 843 384
Victoria Ainsworth, Senior Director (Hawthorn Advisors)
vanquis@hawthornadvisors.com
+44 (0) 7894 995 886
Forward looking statements
This report may contain certain "forward looking statements" regarding the financial position, business strategy or plans for future operations of Vanquis Banking Group. All statements other than statements of historical fact included in this document may be forward looking statements. Forward looking statements also often use words such as "believe", "expect", "estimate", "intend", "anticipate" and words of a similar meaning. By their nature, forward looking statements involve risk and uncertainty that could cause actual results to differ from those suggested by them. Much of the risk and uncertainty relates to factors that are beyond Vanquis Banking Group's ability to control or estimate precisely, such as future market conditions and the behaviours of other market participants, and therefore undue reliance should not be placed on such statements which speak only as at the date of this report. Vanquis Banking Group does not assume any obligation to, and does not intend to, revise or update these forward-looking statements, except as required pursuant to applicable law or regulation. No statement in this announcement is intended as a profit forecast or estimate for any period. No statement in this announcement should be interpreted to indicate a particular level of profit and, as a consequence, it should not be possible to derive a profit figure for any future period from this report.

latest.title: Total Voting Rights
latest.content:
30 Jun 2026 16:30
RNS Number : 4035K
Vanquis Banking Group PLC
30 June 2026
Vanquis Banking Group plc - Voting Rights and Capital
In accordance with the Financial Conduct Authority's Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rule 5.6.1., Vanquis Banking Group plc hereby gives notice that as of 30 June 2026, the company's issued share capital consists of 256,493,758 ordinary shares of 20 8/11p each, with voting rights. Vanquis Banking Group plc does not hold any ordinary shares in Treasury.
The above figure 256,493,758 may be used by shareholders as the denominator for the calculations by which they will determine if they are required to notify their interest in, or a change to their interest in, Vanquis Banking Group plc under the Financial Conduct Authority's Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules.
Elizabeth McClure
Deputy Company Secretary
Tel: 01274 351 351
This information is provided by RNS, the news service of the London Stock Exchange. RNS is approved by the Financial Conduct Authority to act as a Primary Information Provider in the United Kingdom. company_30s.what_they_do: No concrete information about VANQ.L’s core business is available in the supplied data.
company_30s.why_they_matter: Without details on its products or market position, it’s unclear how the company fits into its industry.
company_30s.current_state: Recent filings show routine corporate updates, but there is no sign of a major strategic shift or expansion.

rns_narrative: The latest announcements focus on routine corporate matters: interim results, director shareholding disclosures, and tender‑offer outcomes.
These events suggest the company is maintaining its existing structure rather than pursuing a new growth strategy.
There is no evidence of a major turnaround or product launch in the near term.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Quiet optimism – investors are waiting for a clear catalyst.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: A small group of traders believes the next update could unlock value, citing potential institutional interest.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Others worry that the lack of fresh capital and stagnant activity may signal deeper problems.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The chatter remains steady; no surge in excitement or panic has been observed.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends data is flat, indicating minimal public interest and a high likelihood of surprise if a catalyst emerges.

near_term_catalysts: Release of the upcoming interim results on 6 July could clarify earnings and cash flow.
Any announcement of new institutional investment or a strategic partnership would likely lift sentiment.
A significant uptick in trading volume that breaks recent resistance could signal the start of a recovery.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a sudden spike in volume accompanied by a clear breakout above recent support.
expect_next.timing_regime: Because the depth of the recent decline is unknown, it’s unclear where the stock sits in the recovery cycle. Confirmation would come from a sustained move above key support levels.

buy_vs_sell.lean: Neutral – insufficient data to support a decisive contrarian bullish stance.
buy_vs_sell.bull_case: If the company delivers a positive earnings surprise and attracts institutional buying, the upside could be significant, especially if the price climbs back to levels seen before the recent decline.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: Continued lack of growth signals and potential cash‑burn risk could push the stock further lower if no new capital is raised.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential upside is high if a catalyst triggers a bounce, but the risk of further decline remains because no clear turnaround plan is visible.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter on a clear breakout above recent support, placing a stop just below the most recent low and allocating a modest portion of the portfolio.
playbook.if_watching: Hold cash until the interim results are announced; monitor volume and price action for a definitive reversal.
playbook.if_passing: No imminent terminal risk identified; focus remains on waiting for a catalyst.

ai_insights.bottom_line: VANQ.L sits on a thin information base – a clear catalyst is needed to turn this silent setup into a contrarian opportunity.

Historical data shows 2 rallies averaging 178% upside. Current position: +153.7%. Your edge comes from waiting for AI Engine confirmation instead of blind entry.
commentary.timing_translation: Late stage. Consider taking profits as risk/reward deteriorates.
